refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall update the worksite model within the customer-required timeliness for operational use by minimizing data acquisition latency and processing latency.
refined_fr.rationale: To meet the overall latency requirement, the update process is decomposed into two key sub-functions: acquiring timely data from the worksite and processing that data efficiently to update the model.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure and verify the latency of data acquisition and processing separately and combined, ensuring p95 latency ≤ 1.0 s under supported update scenarios.
sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

candidate_dps: DP1: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with prioritized network protocols
DP2: Implement real-time data processing pipeline with parallel computation
DP3: Optimize data compression and filtering to reduce transmission and processing load
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1 and DP2 combined: Use high-frequency telemetry data streams with prioritized network protocols and implement real-time data processing pipeline with parallel computation

potential_sources: Data acquisition and processing tightly coupled via telemetry interface
coupling_check.notes: DP1 and DP2 are logically coupled but can be modularized with clear interface boundaries.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network link supports required bandwidth and latency
Risk of telemetry data loss impacting update timeliness
Processing pipeline complexity may introduce unforeseen delays
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Monitor network performance and processing load; consider fallback or buffering strategies.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system responsible for data acquisition, processing, and worksite model update

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives high-frequency worksite data streams from Vehicle and Teleoperation modules

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data transmission between Vehicle, Teleoperation, and NGHE Solution modules

module_containment_scores.notes: Centralizes data acquisition and processing, minimizing diffusion and maximizing containment.
Primarily data source; limited processing capability.
Supports remote control; minor role in data processing.
Focused on safety alerts; unrelated to worksite model update latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing the update logic and data processing within the NGHE Solution module will minimize latency and interface complexity.
containment_risks: Potential bottleneck if NGHE Solution module becomes overloaded
Dependency on network link performance may affect containment effectiveness
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Evaluate load balancing and modular decomposition within NGHE Solution if latency targets are not met.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The refined FRs are clearly decomposed and mapped to logical DPs that address latency through data acquisition and processing.

diffusion_risks: Some coupling between telemetry data acquisition and processing may cause diffusion if not well modularized.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Assigning to NGHE Solution module centralizes responsibility and reduces diffusion risk.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by measuring latency metrics at module interfaces and internal processing stages.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of FRs to NGHE Solution module with well-defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Monitor network link performance and consider fallback mechanisms.
Plan for scalability within NGHE Solution to handle processing load.

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment satisfy the latency requirement with clear architectural boundaries and manageable risks.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Acquire worksite data with minimal latency
FR2: Process and update worksite model with minimal latency

revise.why: No immediate revision needed; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Network link supports required bandwidth and latency.
Telemetry data is reliable and timely.
